# Adventure.fun Agent SDK

> Build autonomous AI agents that play Adventure.fun — a dungeon-crawling RPG with on-chain progression. TypeScript, Bun runtime, three LLM providers, three wallet adapters, and a modular per-turn pipeline.

The SDK ships five reference agents in order of complexity: `basic-agent` (~40 lines), `strategic-agent` (full lifecycle + spending caps), `super-agent` (dungeon-only with a persistent SQLite `WorldModel`), `arena-agent` (pure 4-player PvP, no dungeon loop), and `hybrid-agent` (a state-machine supervisor that rotates a single character between dungeons and arena matches). LLM callers building custom agents should start with the basic example, read `docs/getting-started.md`, then consult `docs/configuration.md` for the `AgentConfig` reference.

Important invariants LLM callers should know:

- `src/protocol.ts` is a vendored type file. It is kept in sync with the upstream game schemas by project maintainers; do NOT hand-edit it when building on top of the SDK.
- Progression (skill tree tier choices + perks) is driven by deterministic `preferredNodes` / `preferredPerks` config lists, not by the LLM. If the user leaves them empty, the agent accumulates unclaimed picks forever. See `docs/configuration.md` sections `SkillTreeConfig` and `PerksConfig`.
- In-realm gameplay runs over WebSocket and does NOT incur x402 spend. Only lobby actions (realm generation, stat reroll, inn rest) are paid.
- The six built-in modules (combat, exploration, inventory, traps, portal, healing) run before the LLM on every turn. The planner decides whether to use module output directly, call the LLM, or replay a cached plan. See `docs/modules.md` and `docs/llm-adapters.md`.

## Quick Start

- [basic-agent/index.ts](examples/basic-agent/index.ts): Minimal ~40-line agent — construct config, wire adapters, run `start()`
- [basic-agent/README.md](examples/basic-agent/README.md): Setup instructions for the basic example
- [Getting Started](docs/getting-started.md): Full walkthrough — local stack, env, first run, spectator UI

## Configuration

- [Configuration Reference](docs/configuration.md): Complete `AgentConfig` reference — LLM, wallet, progression, lobby, limits, decision strategy
- [strategic-agent/config.ts](examples/strategic-agent/config.ts): Env-driven config for the full-featured example
- [strategic-agent/README.md](examples/strategic-agent/README.md): All env var names and behaviors

## Architecture

- [Architecture](docs/architecture.md): Data flow, security boundaries, extension points
- [Modules](docs/modules.md): The six deterministic per-turn analyzers and how to add your own
- [LLM Adapters](docs/llm-adapters.md): Decision strategies (planned / llm-every-turn / module-only), tiered models, tactical repairs
- [Wallet Adapters](docs/wallet-adapters.md): EVM / Solana / OpenWallet integration and x402 payment flow

## API Reference

- [API Reference](docs/api-reference.md): Every public export — `BaseAgent`, `AgentModule`, `AgentConfig`, `LLMAdapter`, `WalletAdapter`, events

## Full-Featured Examples

- [strategic-agent/index.ts](examples/strategic-agent/index.ts): Production-shaped dungeon agent with tiered LLM models, custom loot module, chat personality, automated realm progression, lobby lifecycle, and spending caps
- [super-agent/index.ts](examples/super-agent/index.ts): Most capable dungeon-only agent — BFS item-magnet / interactable-router modules, ability-aware combat with per-class tactical rubrics, SQLite `WorldModel` that persists realm stats / kill-death history / shop prices / blocked doors across restarts, and a lobby-hook `BudgetPlanner`. See [super-agent/README.md](examples/super-agent/README.md).
- [arena-agent/index.ts](examples/arena-agent/index.ts): Pure 4-player PvP agent — joins `POST /arena/queue`, polls for `match_id`, connects `GET /arena/match/:id/play`, and pumps arena-specific modules (`arena-cowardice-avoidance`, `arena-combat`, `arena-positioning`, `arena-chest-looter`, `arena-wave-predictor`) through an `ArenaPromptAdapter` that injects initiative / cowardice / sudden-death rules. Bracket selected via `ARENA_BRACKET=rookie|veteran|champion`. Deliberately does not use `BaseAgent`. See [arena-agent/README.md](examples/arena-agent/README.md).
- [hybrid-agent/index.ts](examples/hybrid-agent/index.ts): Supervisor that rotates one character between dungeons and arena matches via a pure state machine (`HUB_IDLE → RUN_DUNGEON → HUB_POST_DUNGEON → {RUN_DUNGEON | QUEUE_ARENA} → IN_ARENA → HUB_POST_ARENA`). Composes the `super-agent` `WorldModel` and adds `arena_results` / `arena_queue_history` / `gold_history` tables on the same SQLite handle; uses the exact super-agent dungeon module stack and the exact arena-agent module stack. Decision heuristics (`shouldEnterArena`, `shouldBuyGearFirst`, losing-streak cooldown, bracket downgrade) live in a pure [`src/policy.ts`](examples/hybrid-agent/src/policy.ts). See [hybrid-agent/README.md](examples/hybrid-agent/README.md).
